analyst_observations: Known-traps version wins all 3 evals on pass rate: 100% vs 69.6% average
The key discriminating assertions are: (1) explicit trap registry check, (2) trap-to-PC mapping table, (3) registry update after forge recycle, (4) dialogService trap for frontend
Old skill still catches most issues via existing invariants (tenant_id, TIMESTAMPTZ, route order) — the gap is SYSTEMATIC coverage vs IMPLICIT coverage
The dialogService trap (trap-002) is the clearest win: old skill missed it entirely on the webhook feature, with_skill caught it via registry
Forge with_skill provides the feedback loop: increments found_count, notes prevention gap, creates institutional memory. Old skill fixes the bug but the learning dies with the session
Token cost is ~20% higher with_skill — acceptable tradeoff for the systematic coverage and learning loop
